observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

a.	The Municipal Accountant strictly follow the accounting treatment for the computation of depreciation expense of the PPE accounts and to prepare the necessary journal entries and revise the financial statements to adjust the errors made pursuant to IPSAS No. 1 and 17.

b.	The Inventory Committee demand the submission of the Report of Physical Count of the abovementioned accounts from the officials charged with the preparation, and reconcile with the accounting records to arrive at the total balances of the PPE accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the agency’s PPEs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has prepared the adjusting entries to record the understatement of depreciation in the books.
observations.reason: The Engineering Office was not able to complete the physical count of the immovable properties and the report submitted was not based on the prescribed format.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to revise the Statement of Financial Performance for CY 2023 to correct the error in recording the foregoing transactions and carefully observe the prescribed guidelines in IPSAS 1 and COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.reason: Some payment of professional fees for services rendered in CY 2024 were paid and recorded as expense only in January 2025

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Project Engineer and the Municipal Health Officer to fast track the implementation of the project; inquire with the Source Agency the reason behind the non-transfer of the remaining balance of the fund; and comply with the lacking and/or additional requirements needed by the Source Agency, if there are any.
observations.reason: The Phase III of the project was not yet completed by the end of the year due to revisions in project design.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Petty Cash Custodian to liquidate on time the cash advances made for the PCF-AICS and request for the replenishment of the fund on a consistent basis, in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.reason: Cash advances for Petty Operating Expenses for CY 2024 were liquidated beyond the timeline prescribed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to review the executed Office Order regarding the Staff Movement at the Office of the Municipal Engineer and observe the guidelines on the movement of human resource relative to the provisions of RA 7160, to ensure the proper and effective discharge of the powers and duties of the officials concerned.
observations.reason: The LCE executed Order No. 34 dated August 29, 2024, for the reassignment and designation of the employees which includes the exchange of office of the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Assessor but failed to include the specific grounds for such reassignment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:

a.    Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of physical inventory taking of all Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) of the Municipality and update the RPCPPE;

b.    Property and Accounting Unit to undertake collaborative procedure to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records and that the Property Cards (PCs) maintained by the Property Unit is reconciled with the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELCs) maintained by the Accounting Unit, taking into consideration the procedural guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006; and

c.    Disposal Committee to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) and initiate the disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.reason:
The physical inventory-taking of the immovable properties of the was not completed by the Inventory Committee

Due to non-completion of the physical count of the immovable properties, the amount of PPE per accounting records did not reconcile with the property records. 

IIRUP was not prepared and the appraisal and disposal of these properties was not yet initiated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management strictly adhere to the guidelines on granting of Representation and Transportation Allowance and to stop the payment of such benefit to non-qualified LGU officials.  

This audit finding is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.
observations.reason: Payments of RATA were still made to non-qualified officials for the period January to May 2024.

observations.managementAction: a. The PESO Manager submitted a written explanation about the deficiencies and observations cited.
observations.reason:
b. The revised MOA was not submitted to the Audit Team



c. This recommendation could not yet be verified since there was no funding for the TUPAD Program for CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to intensify the tax collections and coordinate with other LGU officials to formulate strategies to improve collection of RPT and SET. Likewise, we recommended that management avail all of the legal remedies provided under RA 7160 to collect all taxes due to the LGU, with proper coordination with the Provincial Government.
observations.managementAction: The LGU activated a Municipal Revenue Generation Group to carefully plan about the legal remedies to be put in place to improve collection of RPT and SET.
observations.reason: Low collection efficiency was still observed in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a.    Require the General Services Officer (GSO) and the Disposal Committee to prepare the corresponding Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for the identification of unserviceable properties and initiate the process of disposing the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445 and the rules provided in COA Circular No. 92-386; and

b.    After the disposal, require the GSO to submit the necessary documents, together with the Official Receipt to the Accounting Office, as the basis of the latter to drop the unserviceable properties from the book of accounts in accordance with the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

c.    Require the GSO and Municipal Accountant to investigate the discrepancy of P1,666,186.56 which appears in the inventory report but not in the accounting records.  Likewise, require them to investigate the difference in the costing of certain properties listed in both offices for the appropriate adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The LCE held a second meeting with the Disposal Committee for the application of the manual of the disposal and property.
observations.reason:
a. The LCE has already issued an order to the GSO and the Disposal Committee for the disposal of the unserviceable properties and equipment of the LGU. However, no action was yet taken by the management. 





b. No action was taken by the management.





c. No action was taken by the management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a.    File a request for write -off with the Commission on Audit fully supported with required documents prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005, and

b.    Require the Accountant to provide Allowance for Impairment corresponding to the uncollectible accounts pursuant to Paragraph 67 of IPSAS 29 to reduce the affected receivables accounts to its appropriate carrying amounts;

c.    Require the Accountant to make adjusting entry to correct the balance of Other Receivables account considering the difference of P11,574.83 between the book balance and the Report of Status of Receivables.
observations.managementAction:
b. Allowance for impairment was already set up for the Other Receivables account under the General Fund and SEF.



c. The adjusting entry was already made by the Municipal Accountant
observations.reason: a. Request for write-off was not yet initiated by the LGU due to difficulty of securing documents regarding the receivables.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
The Engineering Office to complete the physical count of the immovable properties and submit to the Audit Team and the Accounting Office the corresponding Report on Physical Count of Immovable PPE.
The Inventory Committee and the Accounting Office reconcile their records based on the results of the physical inventory to arrive at the reconciled balances of the PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
Reconcile the balances of the Due to GSIS, Due to Pag-IBIG and Due to PhilHealth accounts and effect the adjustments in the financial statements, if necessary; and
Consider the preparation and maintenance of individual subsidiary ledgers for all suppliers and employees to ensure accurate and reliable withholding and remittance of taxes, contributions, and loan amortizations.

We recommended that management strictly observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular 2013-004, dated January 30, 2013. A complete list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year should be prepared by the official/s concerned and should be submitted to the Audit Team to facilitate monitoring thereof.

We recommended that the GSO prepare and submit to GIF of GSIS and COA Audit Team, the Property Inventory Form (PIF) listing of all the insurable assets and properties of the Municipality, not later than April 30 of each year, as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2018-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the GAD Focal Person and other officials concerned to carefully identify the GAD Programs and Projects to be included in the GAD Plan and Budget of the LGU, in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to review the accounting records pertaining to the unremitted taxes and immediately remit the same to the BIR in accordance with RMC No. 47-2023, in order to settle the tax liability of the Municipality and avoid possible assessment of deficiency taxes and penalties.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, together with the Human Resource Management Officer, provide the Audit Team with justification for the employment of such Contract of Service employee, carefully consider the necessity of hiring Contract of Service employees, and review the duties/functions provided in their contracts to avoid duplication or overlapping of functions with those performed by the regular employees.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning & Development Officer, the OIC-Municipal Engineer, the Bids and Awards Committee, and other officials concerned, to coordinate with each other to fast track and monitor the completion of the development projects for the immediate use of the constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries, revise the financial statements to correct the effects of the improper computation of the Prepaid Insurance of the Municipality as at December 31, 2023 and observe the prescribed guidelines in IPSAS 1 and COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has prepared the adjusting entries to correct the Prepaid Insurance account in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) provide adequate measures that will facilitate the prompt submission of the Purchase Orders and Perfected Contracts with complete information and supporting documents, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001, to facilitate the timely review, evaluation and detection of possible errors/deficiencies in the contracts.
observations.managementAction: Purchase Orders were made through email to facilitate prompt submission. On the other hand, the Perfect Contracts of the LGU were submitted to the Audit Team on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to formulate policies and guidelines on the grant of financial and burial assistance, ratified by the Sangguniang Bayan, to ensure the validity and propriety of the disbursements made in providing assistance to clients/recipients as well as to ensure judicious use of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The LGU formulated its’ own guidelines on the grant of financial and burial assistance to constituents which was already approved through the passed by the Sangguniang

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

a.	The Local Chief Executive require the Local School Board to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the claim for travelling expenses; and



b.	The Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursement vouchers are supported with complete documentation.
	This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension and Disallowance as a result of our audit.
observations.managementAction:
The Local School Board has complied with the preparation of the required documents for travel claims such as Itinerary of Travel, Travel Authority and Post Activity Report 

Travel expenses charged to SEF for CY 2024 were supported with completed documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, the MENRO-Designate and all other officials concerned to evaluate the present condition of the MRF and RCA and decide on the possible action to be taken to improve the facilities and the solid waste management of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The LGU constructed a new Residual Containment Area which was completed on September 2024 to address issues on solid waste management in the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MDRRM Council through the LDRRMO to carefully plan the proposed programs, projects and activities of the council which shall include all the thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management and observe timely implementation to enhance the emergency capabilities of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The proposed PPAs of the LDRRM Council covered all the thematic areas of disaster risk and reduction and management and were timely implemented during the year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to:

a.	Strictly enforce the terms and conditions of the Contract of Lease and impose the one percent (1%) surcharge from delinquent stallholders and booth occupants as mentioned in the 2022 Revised Municipal Revenue Code of Gamay. 

b.	Observe the timely collection of the unsettled accounts and/or arrears from defaulting stallholders and exhaust all means to collect the rents due.
observations.managementAction: Management already enforced the imposition of one percent (1%) surcharge for late payments of rent by stallholders and booth occupants.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

a.	The Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the GAD Focal Person/s and other officials concerned to provide a budget for Gender and Development activities equivalent to at least 5% of the total appropriations and incorporate it in the Annual Investment Program (AIP) of the LGU in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

b.	The Local Chief Executive (LCE) propose a training program on the HGDG in its’ GPB and/or seek assistance of a gender analysis specialist/expert in the use of and/or administration of the HGDG in the attribution of the LGU programs and activities to the GAD budget as required in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

c.	The GAD Focal Person to furnish the COA Audit Team a copy of the DILG approved Annual GAD Plan and Budget and the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2021 and observe the period of its preparation as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001.
observations.managementAction:
Appropriation for the 5% GAD programs of 
the Municipality were made through attributions.



HGDG training programs were already undertaken by identified LGU employees



The GAD Focal Person submitted the DILG approved GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer:

a.    To facilitate the issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance for the completed projects of which the defects liability had already lapsed, upon satisfaction that the contractors had performed their obligations according to the terms of the contracts; and

b.    To ensure that the same certificate be issued after the lapse of the defects liability period or one year from project completion for all infrastructure projects, pursuant to Section 62.2.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, in order to establish the effectivity period of the warranty security.
observations.managementAction: Certificates of Final Acceptance were issued for eligible LGU projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Supply Office/General Services Office to conduct physical count of the unused/undistributed drugs and medicines and submit a report thereon; The Supply Officer to prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant on a monthly basis the RSMI to provide the basis for the computation of supplies issued and ending inventory; and The Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entry to record the issuance of common-use supplies based on the report of the Supply Officer/GSO.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2019 Finding No. 3 a. The GSO a. Implemented b. No Action c. The Municipal Accountant

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE: a. Require the Property Officer/GSO and the Municipal Accountant to maintain stock cards and property ledger cards, respectively; b. Require the Property Officer/GSO to properly assign unique Property Number to each item of PPE for easy reference; c. Initiate the formulation of plans/strategies for the one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts to come up with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2012 to CY 2019 Finding No. 9 c. The one-time cleansing of PPE accounts was not yet completed despite the conduct of inventory-taking. Also, no reconciliation was made between the GSO and Accounting Office to come up with a reconciled balance in the RPCPPE.
observations.reason: The one-time cleansing of PPE accounts was conducted but reconciliation between GSO and Accounting Office was not completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer: a. To facilitate the issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance for the completed projects where the defects liability had already lapsed, upon satisfaction that the contractors had performed their obligations according to the terms of the contracts; and b. To ensure that the same certificate be issued after the lapse of the defects liability period or one year from project completion for all infrastructure projects, in order to establish the effectivity period of the warranty security.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. Require the GSO and the Disposal Committee to prepare the corresponding Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for the identification of unserviceable properties and initiate the process of disposing the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445 and the rules provided in COA Circular No. 92-386; b. After the disposal, require the GSO to submit the necessary documents, together with the Official Receipt to the Accounting Office, as the basis of the latter to drop the unserviceable properties from the book of accounts in accordance with the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations; c. Require the GSO and Municipal Accountant to investigate the discrepancy of P1,666,186.56 which appears in the inventory report but not in the accounting records. Likewise, require them to investigate the difference in the costing of certain properties listed in both offices for the appropriate adjustments.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the management. The LCE has issued an order to the GSO and the Disposal Committee for the disposal of the unserviceable properties and equipment of the LGU. However, no action was yet taken by management to complete the disposal process and effect accounting drop.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. File a request for write-off with the Commission on Audit fully supported with the required documents prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005; b. Require the Accountant to provide Allowance for Impairment corresponding to the uncollectible accounts pursuant to Paragraph 67 of IPSAS 29 to reduce the affected receivables to its appropriate carrying amounts; c. Require the Accountant to make adjusting entry to correct the balance of Other Receivables account considering the difference of P11,574.83 between the book balance and the Report of Status of Receivables.
observations.managementAction: a. Request for write-off was not yet initiated by the LGU. b. Allowance for impairment was set up for the Other Receivables account under the General Fund. c. Adjusting entries were made by the Municipal Accountant to correct balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the GAD focal person to maximize the utilization of GAD based funds through the implementation of programs and projects addressing issues on women’s empowerment and gender equality, targeting optimum number of results/outputs so that the maximum number of intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection, and other assistance that may be provided.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2021, GAD PPAs could not be verified because the GAD Plan and Budget together with the Accomplishment Report were not submitted to the Audit Team on time. The LGU committed to submit the GAD Accomplishment Report as soon as available.
observations.reason: The GAD Accomplishment Report / Utilization report was not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
2.9 In view of the above deficiencies, we recommended the Local Chief Executive
require the:

e. Inventory Committee to conduct complete annual physical inventory-taking of
PPE using the guidelines enumerated in COA Circular No. 2020-006 and
submit a copy of the RPCPPE/Inventory Report not later than January 31 of
each year using the prescribe format in Annex A of the Circular;
f. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the PPE Ledger Cards
(PPELC) regularly reconciled with the General Ledger and Inventory Report
including other property records like the Property Acknowledgement Receipt
(PAR). The GSO-designate and the Municipal Accountant should update
property records based on the procedural guidelines provided in COA Circular
No. 2020-006 to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts to be
considered as the correct balance of the LGU’s PPE; and
g. The Inventory Committee to complete the one-time cleansing process of the
LGU’s PPE and update property records to come up with the reconciled
balances of PPE accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the
LGU’s PPEs.

h. The Municipal Accountant to provide additional information of the PPE
accounts in the notes, especially those showing significant amounts.
Likewise, the notes should also indicate the nature, composition or
significant changes, if any.

3.7 We recommended that:

d) The Municipal Accountant and GSO/Municipal Treasurer record regular
purchases of inventories using the perpetual inventory method;
e) The GSO/Municipal Treasurer conduct regular count of inventories on
hand; Prepare the required inventory reports, schedules, supplies ledger
cards, stock cards and use/accomplish the prescribed inventory forms; and
f) The Municipal Accountant ensures coordination between the end-users,
GSO and the accounting office for the submission of required reports and
the proper accounting for the inventories.

4.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to intensify the tax collections and coordinate with other LGU
officials to formulate strategies to improve collection of RPT and SET.
Likewise, we recommended that management avail all of the legal remedies
provided under RA 7160 to collect all taxes due to the LGU, with proper
coordination with the Provincial Government.

5.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to submit the needed documents to validate the grant of Special
Risk Allowance to some of the health workers and “Emergency Hired
Volunteers”. Henceforth, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor and
other officials concerned ensure that the LGU strictly comply with the
pertinent laws, rules, and regulations when granting similar benefits to
personnel in order to maintain efficiency and propriety in the utilization of
government funds.

6.4 We recommended that the management should immediately initiate an
action to enforce settlement of disallowance in accordance with the COA
Revised Rules of Procedures on the Settlement of Accounts (RSSA).

7.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to release to the Barangays on a
quarterly basis, the shares of the barangays from RPT collections to augment
the funds for their operations.

8.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant and other officials/employees concerned to comply with the
requirements on the submission of official receipts and other acceptable
forms of evidence for all government expenditures in compliance with COA
Circular No. 2004-006.

9.10 We recommended that:

a. The Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the GAD Focal Person/s and
other officials concerned to provide a budget for Gender and
Development activities equivalent to at least 5% of the total
appropriations and incorporate it in the Annual Investment Program
(AIP) of the LGU in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
b. The Local Chief Executive (LCE) propose a training program on the
HGDG in its’ GPB and/or seek assistance of a gender analysis
specialist/expert in the use of and/or administration of the HGDG in the
attribution of the LGU programs and activities to the GAD budget as
required in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2013-01.
c. The GAD Focal Person to furnish the COA Audit Team a copy of the
DILG approved Annual GAD Plan and Budget and the corresponding
GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2021 and observe the period of its
preparation as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001.

10.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:

a. Require the GSO and other offices concerned to provide the accounting
unit of the copies of the RIS/distribution list of distributed/donated goods,
supplies and equipment.
b. Ensure the submission of the Monthly Report on the Receipt and
Distribution/Donation of Goods/Supplies/Equipment.

11.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:

a. LDRRM Officer to optimize the utilization of the capital outlay
component of LDRRM Fund;
b. LDRRM Officer and Municipal Budget Officer to avoid the inclusion of
ineligible programs/projects/activities (PPAs), instead, prioritize PPAs
for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, and
rehabilitation and recovery; and
c. Municipal Accountant to provide complete disclosure in the Notes to
Financial Statements as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002.

12.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning
& Development Officer, Municipal Engineer, the Bids and Awards Committee,
and other implementers to coordinate with each other to fast track and
monitor the immediate completion of these development projects, ensuring
their usability, sound structure, and availability to the general public or its
intended beneficiaries, so that these projects can contribute to the socio-
economic recovery of the municipality and its people in these difficult times.

13.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that actions needed
to be taken for the immediate release by the Source Agency and
disbursement of future grants and benefits to health workers, or any other
group of beneficiaries, be accomplished with urgency so that recipients of
such grants will be able to immediately benefit from the purpose for which
the funds are intended.

We recommended that the LCE require the Accountant to prepare adjusting journal entry to transfer the amount of P1,695,871.80 from the General Fund to the Trust Fund to correct the balance of Trust Liability- LDRRM account. Henceforth, transfer the yearly unutilized balance of LDRRM funds at the end of the year to the Trust Fund account to reflect the correct balance of the Trust Liability account at every accounting period.
observations.managementAction: Unutilized funds were already transferred to Trust Fund account on February 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to take up the unrecorded receivables at CY 2019 P433,440.00, henceforth, observe the accrual basis of accounting in recording transactions to give a more accurate picture of the agency’s assets, liabilities and equity accounts in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were made by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entry to recognize the PPE accounts in the amount of P74,446.00, and thus observe the proper recording of acquisition of properties and equipment pursuant to IPSAS 17 and COA Circular 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already prepared the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the journal entry to adjust/correct the net carrying value of the infrastructure projects, and henceforth, observe the provision in IPSAS 17 on the computation of depreciation which should begin when assets are available for use.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already prepared the necessary adjusting entries pursuant to IPSAS 17.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting entry to reclassify the completed slaughterhouse in the amount of P2,985,502.00 from Buildings to Slaughterhouse account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already prepared the necessary adjusting entries pursuant to COA Circular 2015-009.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Bids and Awards Committee to submit the lacking supporting documents and thus ensure that documentary requirements in the utilization of 20% DF and funds coming from various agencies for CY 2021 are complied with and that the contracts for the procurement of goods and services are awarded to suppliers/contractors with the necessary safeguards for the prevention of abuses and corruption in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) already complied with the mandatory documentary requirements in the utilization of the 20% DF and funds coming from various agencies for CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require: a. The BAC Secretariat to post the accumulated reports on utilization of Bayanihan Grant in at least three (3) conspicuous public places and submit the said reports or the information on the compliance to posting and reporting requirements to other offices where submission is required; b. The designated officials/employees to undertake immediately the required inspection and acceptance upon the delivery of goods and services to ensure that the goods delivered comply with the specifications in the contract/PO, and be immediately delivered or issued to end-users/beneficiaries especially in times of emergencies/calamities.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat already complied with the posting and reporting requirements for Emergency Procurement. Also, inspection and acceptance of goods were made on time and delivered to end-users/beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to install measures that will facilitate the submission of the perfected Contracts and their supporting documents within the prescribed period to facilitate legal and auditorial review, technical evaluation and inspection pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: The LGU already complied with the timeline set for the submission of perfected Contracts. In addition, the LGU installed a monitoring system for the submission of the perfected contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE advise the Municipal Treasurer to strictly require all the Revenue Collectors to turn-over daily and intact, all collections. Also, require the Municipal Treasurer to verify the Report of Collections submitted by all accountable officers, count the money turned over to her, sign the certification and receipt portion and deposit intact all collections.
observations.managementAction: The LGU already installed a monitoring system for the daily turnover of collections. The functions of the employees were already included in the IPCR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers complete with relevant supporting documents and keenly observe the deadline set on the submission of monthly trial balances and other financial reports.
observations.managementAction: On time submission of reports and accounts were observed by the LGU for CY 2021. The LGU also installed a monitoring system for the prompt submission of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the LGU Officials concerned: a. To fast track the implementation of the Capital Outlay PPA of the DRRM funds; and b. Henceforth implement all LDRRM PPAs as programmed in the AIP and LDRRM Investment Plan in order to enhance the LGU’s pre-disaster prevention and mitigation capability in times of calamities.
observations.managementAction: The capital outlay programs for CY 2021 were implemented in CY 2022. The LGU will fast track implementation of outstanding LDRRM PPAs in CY 2022.
observations.reason: Some LDRRM PPAs were implemented in CY 2022; not all LDRRM PPAs were implemented in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE through the Municipal Assessor coordinate with the Provincial Assessor so that the CY 2013 General Revision of Assessments and Property Classification be made possible and enable the LGU to collect higher amount of Real Property and Special Education Taxes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor already updated the General Revision of Assessments and Property Classification for the collection of RPT and SET. However, still the LGU has a low collection efficiency during the year.
observations.reason: Although revisions were made on the Assessment and Property Classification for taxable RPUs, the collection efficiency remained low during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the management to: a) submit justification/explanation on the discrepancies noted; b) direct the Accountant and the one in charge of processing claims for payment to carefully check the completeness of supporting documents particularly the lacking information and data required to be indicated thereof; c) strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR in the conduct of procurement activities.
observations.managementAction: The LGU observed the proper conventions on the documentary requirements to support procurement of goods and services. The required documents were provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require local officials to support all issuance of construction materials with the necessary documents to document and prove the actual repair and maintenance undertaken. Likewise, we recommend for the submission of the required pre and post inspection reports and other documents to validate the projects specified herein.
observations.managementAction: Disbursement vouchers submitted were supported with the pre and post inspection reports. The required documents were provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Human Resource Management Officer and Municipal Accountant require all Job Order personnel to submit Job Order Contracts as well as the other requirement as basis for the preparation of payrolls. Likewise, the accountant should require the attachment of biometric print out in lieu or in support of DTR and certifications pursuant to Section 4.1.3(b) and (c) of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.reason: Registration of Job Order employees with the biometric system is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should workout to have an approved 10-year solid waste management plan pursuant to the provisions of sections 16 and 17 of RA 9003. Likewise, we recommend that management strictly enforce the other relevant provisions with emphasis on Sec. 20 of the said law in order to attain a systematic and comprehensive solid waste management for the protection of public health and the environment.
observations.reason: The LGU has already an approved Solid Waste Management Plan. However the Solid Waste Management Diversion as provided in Section 20 of RA No. 9003 and as contained in the LGU’s SWMP was not fully implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management should appropriate in detail of particular amounts for each DRRM PPAs in an Appropriation Ordinance/Annual Budget.  Otherwise, the LCE should secure authority from the SB every time DRRM PPAs is procured.

This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance in audit.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:

a.	Prepare and issue Notices and/or Demand Letters through all possible means to all accountable former officials and employees of the LGU, coordinate with officials of other LGUs in locating their whereabouts and engage the services of the official courier (Philippine Postal Corporation) if necessary.  

b.	Prepare and issue Notices and/or Demand Letters through all possible means to the beneficiaries of the livelihood program, and coordinate with Barangay Officials in order to locate their whereabouts

c.	Prepare a Schedule and Analysis to determine which of these accounts could qualify for write off.

We recommend that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to remit to the provincial government for its Local School Board all the unremitted amounts and henceforth, remit to the said school board its share automatically and intact upon collection.

We recommend that the LCE require the GAD focal person to prepare the yearly GAD Plan and Budget, and GAD Accomplishment Report   pursuant to DBM, NEDA and NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-01 and the General Appropriations Act, so that GAD related PPAs could be effectively carried out and evaluated for the good and benefit of the intended beneficiaries. We likewise recommend that management issue appropriate Agency Guidelines in order to come up with a good and workable GAD Plan.

We recommend that that the Local Chief Executive reactivate the municipal LCPC, its membership and appropriate the amount corresponding to 1% of the LGU’s IRA that will shoulder the LCPC’s programs, projects and activities for the protection welfare of children. We recommend further that the LCPC be pro-active in carrying out the relevant programs, projects and activities as envisioned in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120, implement other pertinent national and local laws in order to attain optimum benefits for the children specially the delinquents and disadvantaged.
observations.reason: The LCE has ordered the Mun. Budget Officer to appropriate the amount of corresponding to 1% of the LGUs IRA. The inclusion was done in CY 2019 Budget because during the time the AOM was received, the Budget for CY 2018 was already prepared and approved.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004. The required information for on-going PPAs should be posted on signboards to be displayed outdoor in conspicuous places at the LGU compound or at the project location in order to inform the public regarding matters of public concern.

We recommend that the LCE require the Accountant to remit taxes withheld promptly and regularly in pursuance to the provisions of RA 8424 and the applicable BIR Rules and Regulations to address the thrust of the national government of prompt collection of taxes for its national programs.
observations.reason: While prior years’ unremitted taxes were already remitted accordingly, the current year’s unremitted withholding taxes has rather increased to ₱1,026,021.83.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Property and Supply Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer for the submission of the documents relative to the receipt and distribution of the relief goods to facilitate accounting and reporting thereof, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-002.  Henceforth, all receipts and issuances of relief goods and property shall be undertaken by the Property and Supply Office.  All goods shall be sorted, inventoried, counted and recorded upon receipt before repacking.  Issuance and distribution of goods shall be based on the prescribed Issuance Form duly approved by the Municipal Mayor or his authorized representative pursuant to existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: Coordinate with COA or PSWDO as to the correct forms and standard operating procedure in receipt distribution of relief goods.
observations.reason: Lack of Technical Knowledge

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit lacking documents as required in Section 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to settle suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management is on the process of complying the requirement.
observations.reason: The documentation is a long process.

Likewise, we recommend that Savings Deposit accounts be classified from Cash in Bank - LCCA to Cash in Bank – LCSA.

We recommend that management conduct physical count of all Property, Plant and Equipment and Investment Property and render a complete and valid report thereon duly classified by type of property and equipment and reconciled with the accounting records, to ascertain the accuracy of PPE and Investment Property accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that the LCE require the appropriate municipal officials to initiate the processing of the TCT for all acquired real properties, in the name of the LGU under the Torrens Title System, so that the LGU shall have clean title and the assets are safeguarded from possible third party claims.  The needed documents should be compiled for each property and be submitted as soon as possible to the appropriate government offices, for such purpose.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had substantially complied with the preparation and submission of the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all funds of the municipality.

Henceforth, she should strictly observe Section 111 of PD 1445.

We recommend that management comply strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances; and require the accountable officers to liquidate the overdue cash advances. Invoke the provisions of law authorizing the withholding of salary of personnel who unjustifiably fails to settle his cash advances within the prescribed period.

We recommend that Management stop the practice of equating the municipal share on the monthly premium contributions to the HDMF with the personal share of employees whose contributions exceed the maximum amount of P100.00 per employee per month.
